prompt: Build a design brief for adding a small repo-local Python command.
required_checks: load the software reference sections needed for Build mode
load the Python extension when project signals match
state boundaries, dependency direction, state ownership, rejected abstractions, and validation
forbidden_behaviors: duplicate security or test-quality specialist workflows
grader: rubric: design brief changes implementation decisions and delegates out-of-scope specialist concerns
ip_handling: original synthetic prompt; no third-party text

prompt: Review a shell script refactor for design quality.
required_checks: load the shell-script extension when signals match
cite SD or extension smell codes
include verification-layer tags and smallest useful correction
forbidden_behaviors: treat style-only preferences as design blockers
skip mandatory extension validation when available
grader: rubric: findings are evidence-backed design issues with calibrated severity and extension context
ip_handling: original synthetic prompt; no third-party text

prompt: Extract the current software design baseline from an existing module before planning changes.
required_checks: inspect existing code structure before recommendations
identify coupling hotspots and semantic drift
preserve compliant existing design choices
forbidden_behaviors: rewrite specialist API, UI, infrastructure, security, or test-quality guidance
grader: rubric: baseline reflects current repo evidence and produces a bounded next move
ip_handling: original synthetic prompt; no third-party text

prompt: Build a design brief for replacing a three-branch conditional with Strategy before any second implementation exists.
required_checks: load the pattern catalog because a named pattern is proposed
name the current force before recommending a pattern
recommend direct code or deletion when the force is too small
state the likely SD-W or SD-C risk introduced by Strategy ceremony
forbidden_behaviors: recommend Strategy because it is a known pattern
omit the Lean value and waste tradeoff
grader: rubric: answer treats design patterns as tactics for current force, not as goals
ip_handling: original synthetic prompt; no third-party text

prompt: Review a patch that adds a payment Adapter and a pass-through UserRepository wrapper.
required_checks: load the pattern catalog because pattern-shaped code is in scope
distinguish a useful external-vocabulary adapter from pass-through repository ceremony
cite existing SD smell families rather than inventing a new pattern namespace
include verification-layer tags
forbidden_behaviors: treat every named pattern as equally good
flag Adapter solely because it adds a class
approve Repository solely because it is a familiar pattern
grader: rubric: findings connect pattern fit to boundary, coupling, waste, and semantic evidence
ip_handling: original synthetic prompt; no third-party text

prompt: Review a Rust patch that adds a workspace facade crate, a public trait with one implementation, feature-gated behavior, and async background tasks.
required_checks: load extensions/rust.md when Cargo and .rs signals match
cite rust.SD-* or core SD-* codes for actual boundary/coupling evidence
separate Rust platform facts from design judgment
include validation and delegation limits
forbidden_behaviors: treat Rust syntax or traits as smells by themselves
review test assertions instead of design
grader: rubric: findings connect Rust crate/workspace evidence to software-design risks without stealing security or test-quality scope
ip_handling: original synthetic prompt; no third-party text
